records.ety: [OE. idole, F. idole, L. idolum, fr. Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ that which is seen, the form, shape, figure, fr. <?/ to see. See Wit, and cf. Eidolon.]

defs: An image or representation of anything.
quotes: Do her adore with sacred reverence, As th' idol of her maker's great magnificence.

defs: An image of a divinity; a representation or symbol of a deity or any other being or thing, made or used as an object of worship; a similitude of a false god.
quotes: That they should not worship devils, and idols of gold.

defs: That on which the affections are strongly (often excessively) set; an object of passionate devotion; a person or thing greatly loved or adored.
quotes: The soldier's god and people's idol.

defs: A false notion or conception; a fallacy.
